hypothesis: An auxiliary per-atom partial-charge head trained jointly with energy/force objectives under a differentiable charge-consistency constraint (e.g., total charge conservation and local charge equilibration) will improve transferability and MD stability for charged and ionic systems (such as ions in explicit solvent) relative to charge-agnostic models trained only on neutral SPICE2 data, as evidenced by lower energy/force MAE and reduced energy drift in long MD runs across varying dielectric environments.
